relation_hints.note: Optimized NPs are a specific type of Lzp-PLGA-NPs.
The Box-Behnken design explores variants of Lzp-PLGA-NPs.
Formulation 11 is one of the DOE formulations.

evidence_spans.supporting_text: Formulation and Optimization of Polymeric Nanoparticles for Intranasal Delivery of Lorazepam Using Box-Behnken Design: In Vitro and In Vivo Evaluation
Effect of four independent factors, that is, polymer, surfactant, drug, and aqueous/organic ratio, was studied on two dependent responses, that is, 𝑧-average and % drug entrapment.
The developed NPs showed nearly spherical shape with 𝑧-average 167–318 d⋅nm, PDI below 0.441, and −18.4 mV zeta potential with maximum % drug entrapment of 90.1%.
prolonged drug release of 69.5 ± 0.8% from optimized NPs up to 24 h.
Lzp-PLGA-NPs were successfully developed by nanoprecipitation method using PLGA as polymer, poloxamer as surfactant and acetone as organic phase.
Lorazepam was purchased from R L Fine Chem., Bangalore, India.
poloxamer 407 were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich, St. Louis, USA.
HPLC grade acetone and water were purchased from Fisher Scientific, Mumbai, India.
NPs were characterized for particle size, zeta potential, % drug entrapment
In vitro cell viability study on Vero cell line confirmed the safety of optimized NPs.
Box-Behnken design was employed for constructing polynomial model for optimization of Lzp-PLGA-NPs keeping 4 independent and 2 dependent variables using Design Expert
The independent and dependent variables are listed in Table 1.
Table 1: Independent and dependent variables levels in Box-Behnken design.
During the process, the organic phase was prepared by dissolving accurately weighed PLGA and lorazepam in acetone as organic solvent.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: 2.5. Drug Entrapment Efficiency and Percentage Drug Loading
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Entrapment efficiency of lorazepam was calculated by determining the amount of free drug present in supernatant through HPLC method.
aqueous phase containing surfactant (poloxamer 407) dissolved in water as aqueous solvent.

zeta potential was measured using Malvern Zetasizer
Average particle size (z-average) and polydispersity index (PDI) of the developed nanoparticles were determined by laser dynamic light scattering using Malvern Zetasizer

unassigned_observations.note: Table 1 reports coded levels for cPF, cPVA, cPLGA, and pH, but the actual concentrations corresponding to these coded levels are not provided in the sample rows.

The provided summary for 'table_02' in the prompt appears to describe content from Table 5 or Table 6 of the paper text (release data parameters), not Table 2 (coded values and measured responses of factors). It was excluded from extraction due to potential mislabeling and lack of clear formulation boundaries for component extraction.

Formulations F2-F10, F12-F13, F15-F18, F21-F25 are implied by the 'F-numbered rows' pattern in Table 1 but not explicitly sampled in the prompt. Their properties would follow the same structure as F1, F14, F26.
